CloudVista Systems

Enterprise infrastructure simplified through intelligent cloud automation

Now Hiring: Lead Cloud Engineer

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly; 2 days/week on-site) · Full-time · $185,000 - $235,000 USD annually

C

loudVista Systems seeks an experienced Lead Cloud Engineer to architect and oversee cloud infrastructure

strategy for our fastest-growing customer accounts. This role combines hands-on technical leadership with

team mentorship, requiring deep expertise in AWS cloud design patterns, Infrastructure-as-Code, and cost

optimization. You will lead a team of 4-5 engineers, make critical architectural decisions, and drive adoption of

best practices across our platform engineering organization.

A B O U T U S

CloudVista Systems is a rapidly growing cloud infrastructure and automation company founded in 2018, serving

mid-to-enterprise clients across financial services, healthcare, and technology sectors. We specialize in designing,

deploying, and optimizing multi-cloud architectures with a focus on cost efficiency and operational excellence.

Our team of 120+ engineers is distributed across North America and Europe, and we pride ourselves on a culture

of technical depth and continuous learning.

Y O U R R O L E

Lead and mentor a team of 4-5 cloud engineers, conducting code reviews, design discussions, and career
development planning to ensure technical growth and accountability

Design and oversee deployment of highly available, secure, and cost-optimized AWS architectures for large-

scale enterprise customers, utilizing EC2, RDS, ECS/EKS, Lambda, and VPC configurations

Drive Infrastructure-as-Code strategy using Terraform and CloudFormation, establishing standards, automation

pipelines, and version control workflows across the team

Architect multi-region disaster recovery and high-availability solutions, implementing cross-AZ failover, load

balancing, and backup strategies

Establish and implement cloud cost optimization initiatives, including Reserved Instance strategies, spot

instance orchestration, and right-sizing recommendations to reduce customer cloud spend by 20-30%

Partner with Solutions Architecture and Sales teams to evaluate customer requirements, estimate infrastructure

costs, and develop proposals for complex cloud migrations and modernizations

Troubleshoot and resolve critical production incidents involving networking, IAM policies, Lambda scaling,

and database performance; lead blameless post-mortems

 Champion security and compliance best practices, including VPC isolation, IAM role least-privilege design,

encryption at rest and in transit, and CIS benchmark adherence

Evaluate and integrate emerging tools such as GitOps platforms, observability solutions, and container
orchestration frameworks into the platform roadmap

R E Q U I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

6+ years of hands-on experience with AWS cloud infrastructure, with demonstrated expertise in EC2, VPC,

IAM, RDS, Lambda, and S3

4+ years of professional experience writing and maintaining Infrastructure-as-Code using Terraform and/or

CloudFormation

Proven experience leading or mentoring a team of 3+ engineers, with demonstrated ability to develop talent,

delegate effectively, and build psychological safety

Deep understanding of networking concepts including VPC design, subnetting, routing, security groups,

NACLs, VPN, and AWS PrivateLink

Strong proficiency in Python or Go for scripting, automation, and infrastructure tooling

Expert-level knowledge of AWS IAM policy design, role assumption, cross-account architectures, and

permission boundaries

Experience with cost analysis, billing optimization, and implementing tagging strategies and budgeting controls

D E S I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

AWS certification (Solutions Architect Professional or DevOps Engineer Professional)

Experience with container orchestration platforms (ECS, EKS, Kubernetes) and container security

Hands-on experience with multi-cloud deployments (Azure or GCP) or hybrid cloud architecture

Background in enterprise infrastructure migration projects with measurable business outcomes

Familiarity with observability and monitoring tools (CloudWatch, Datadog, New Relic, Prometheus)

Open source contribution history or active involvement in DevOps/cloud automation communities

T O O L S  O F  T H E T R A D E

AWS (EC2, RDS, VPC, IAM, Lambda, ECS/EKS, S3, CloudWatch, CloudFormation), Terraform, Python, Git &

GitLab CI/CD, Docker & Kubernetes, Linux/Unix system administration, Networking protocols & AWS
networking services, Cost optimization & billing analytics tools

W E O F F E R

Competitive salary with annual performance bonuses and equity (stock options) for all full-time employees

 Comprehensive health insurance including medical, dental, vision, and mental health coverage; employer

covers 85% of premiums

Professional development budget of $3,000 per year for conferences, certifications, training courses, and AWS
exam reimbursement

Flexible work environment with 2 days/week on-site expectations and unlimited PTO policy

Parental leave (12 weeks paid), sabbatical program (4 weeks after 5 years), and emergency backup childcare

401(k) matching up to 5% of salary; ESPP with 10% discount

Home office equipment allowance ($1,500 one-time) and monthly internet stipend

Company-wide quarterly in-person gatherings, engineering summits, and team building activities

T O A P P LY

Interested candidates should submit a resume, cover letter, and a brief description of a recent cloud infrastructure

project you led (1-2 paragraphs) to careers@cloudvista.com with subject line 'Lead Cloud Engineer Application -

[Your Name]'. Please include links to any relevant GitHub repositories, published articles, or open source

contributions. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.
